summary: Decompiled two Super Scope item functions, `it_80291F14` and `it_80291FA8`, in `itsscope.c`. The PR also refined Super Scope attribute layout by replacing padding at offset `+0xC` with a `float xC[10]` array, enabling charge-level indexed cost lookups. Function prototypes in `itsscope.h` were updated from unknown placeholders to typed signatures, including a typed declaration for `it_80291D38`. The PR body reports 100% objdiff matches and `main.dol: OK` SHA1 verification.
what_changed: Added `#include "itsscopebeam.h"` to `itsscope.c`, needed for the call to `it_80298DEC` used by the decompiled firing/beam helper path.
Replaced the placeholder `/// #it_80291F14` with a typed implementation `void it_80291F14(Item_GObj* gobj, int charge_level)`.

`it_80291F14` retrieves `Item*` via `GET_ITEM`, obtains `itSScopeAttributes*` via `GET_ATTRS`, selects a cost from `attrs->xC[charge_level]` for charge levels 0 through 9, subtracts that cost from `ip->xD4C`, clamps `ip->xD4C` to zero, and uses `PAD_STACK(16)` for matching.
Replaced the placeholder `/// #it_80291FA8` with `void it_80291FA8(Item_GObj* gobj, Vec3* pos, int charge_level, float scale)`.
`it_80291FA8` computes cost with `it_80291D38(gobj, charge_level)`, subtracts and clamps `ip->xD4C`, then calls `it_80298DEC(ip->owner, pos, charge_level, scale)` before `PAD_STACK(8)`.
Updated `itSScopeAttributes` in `itCommonItems.h` so offset `+0xC` is modeled as `float xC[10]` instead of `char pad_xC[0x28]`.

Updated `itsscope.h` prototypes: `it_80291D38` became `int it_80291D38(Item_GObj*, int)`, `it_80291F14` became `void it_80291F14(Item_GObj*, int)`, and `it_80291FA8` became `void it_80291FA8(Item_GObj*, Vec3*, int, float)`.
smart_moves: Converted anonymous struct padding into a typed `float xC[10]` array only where the decompiled code needed indexed access, preserving the known offsets while exposing meaningful data layout.
Used explicit charge-level switch cases rather than over-simplifying into a single array access; this likely preserved compiler control-flow/codegen needed for a 100% match.
Kept resource accounting behavior clear: subtract a charge-dependent cost from `ip->xD4C` and clamp negative values back to zero in both decompiled functions.
Added the specific beam header include instead of relying on unknown declarations, allowing `it_80298DEC` to be called with a typed interface.
Used `PAD_STACK(16)` and `PAD_STACK(8)` in the two functions to satisfy stack layout/codegen constraints while keeping the core logic readable.
decomp_lessons: When a function indexes into what was previously padding, update the shared item attribute struct with the narrowest evidenced type and size; here `pad_xC[0x28]` became `float xC[10]` at the same offset.

For matching, do not assume an obvious simplification such as `cost = attrs->xC[charge_level]` will produce matching code. The accepted version preserved a switch with separate handling for charge levels 0, 1-8, and 9.
After decompiling item functions, immediately replace `UNK_RET` and `UNK_PARAMS` prototypes in the item header with typed signatures so later functions can build on them.
Item logic that spends ammo/charge often needs explicit clamping after subtraction; `ip->xD4C` was guarded against going below zero in both functions.
Stack padding macros remain an accepted matching tactic for small item functions when otherwise-correct C does not reproduce the exact stack frame.
naming_conventions: Kept existing address-based symbol names for unknown/decompiled Super Scope functions: `it_80291F14`, `it_80291FA8`, `it_80291D38`, and `it_80298DEC`.
Used `gobj` for `Item_GObj*` parameters and `ip` for the retrieved `Item*`, consistent with common item code style.
Named the charge argument `charge_level`, which documents the role of the integer used for Super Scope charge/cost indexing.
Named the exposed attribute array `xC` to reflect its struct offset while avoiding unsupported semantic naming.
Preserved existing item attribute type name `itSScopeAttributes`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK(16)` in `it_80291F14` and `PAD_STACK(8)` in `it_80291FA8` to match stack behavior.
Modeled `attrs->xC` as a float array and cast selected elements to `int` before subtracting from `ip->xD4C`, matching the apparent generated code behavior.
Retained a switch statement over `charge_level` with grouped cases 1 through 8 and explicit cases 0 and 9, rather than collapsing the logic.
Verified by objdiff according to the PR body: all functions at 100% match.
Verified final binary according to the PR body: `main.dol: OK` with SHA1 verification.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that give semantic names to `it_80291F14`, `it_80291FA8`, `it_80291D38`, or `it_80298DEC`.
Search for other uses of `itSScopeAttributes::xC` to determine whether the float array represents ammo cost, charge cost, or beam power values.
Compare other charge-based item decompilations for the same subtract-and-clamp pattern on `ip->xD4C`.
Look for subsequent cleanup replacing the switch in `it_80291F14` with a simpler indexed form if compiler constraints changed.
